package handler

import (
	"net/http"

	"example.com/app/repository"
	"example.com/app/model"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
)

// Positive scenario for cross_layer_import_violation_by_package_name.
// Rule intent: Handlers (transport layer) should not import repository or model packages directly.
// They should go through a service layer. This violates layered architecture.
// Anti-pattern: Handler imports repository package directly, bypassing the service layer.

type UserHandler struct {
	userRepo *repository.UserRepository
	orderRepo *repository.OrderRepository
}

func NewUserHandler() *UserHandler {
	return &UserHandler{
		userRepo:  repository.NewUserRepository(),
		orderRepo: repository.NewOrderRepository(),
	}
}

func (h *UserHandler) GetUser(c *gin.Context) {
	id := c.Param("id")

	// Cross-layer violation: handler directly calls repository
	user, err := h.userRepo.FindByID(id)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusNotFound, gin.H{"error": "user not found"})
		return
	}

	orders, err := h.orderRepo.FindByUserID(user.ID)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "failed to fetch orders"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{
		"user":   user,
		"orders": orders,
	})
}

func (h *UserHandler) DeleteUser(c *gin.Context) {
	id := c.Param("id")

	// Cross-layer violation: handler directly calls repository
	err := h.userRepo.Delete(id)
	if err != nil {
		c.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, gin.H{"error": "delete failed"})
		return
	}

	c.JSON(http.StatusOK, gin.H{"deleted": true})
}
